results.possibleIdentity: Chester L. "Chet" Sandberg is an engineer and technical consultant who began his professional career at Westinghouse Research in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, in 1967. He graduated from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) with a Bachelor of Science degree in 1967 and later received a Master of Science degree in Electrical Engineering from Stanford University in 1972. During his time at Westinghouse Research, his work involved diverse technical fields including industrial automation. In 1972, he joined the Chemelex Division of Raychem Corporation (now part of nVent/Tyco), where he spent 30 years in various leadership roles, including Chief Engineer and Technical Strategy Manager. His technical expertise spans electrical and mechanical engineering, electrical heat tracing, fiber optic sensors, and conductive polymer heating elements. Sandberg has been highly active in professional societies, serving as an IEEE Fellow (elevated in 1999) and a member of the American Society of Mechanical Engineers (ASME). He has authored or co-authored over 50 technical papers and holds 9 patents. After retiring from Raychem in 2002, he founded C L Sandberg & Associates, where he has consulted on advanced energy technologies such as lithium-ion batteries and energy storage systems. His association with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) is evidenced by technical profile records that match his background as a Westinghouse researcher interested in cross-disciplinary collaboration during the late 1960s.
